title: Two Teenagers Charged Following Stabbing At Bondi Beach | 10 News
description: Two teenagers have been charged, accused of stabbing a man on a street in Sydney's Bondi. The pair were both allegedly on bail at the time of the offence.  Witnesses claim one of the boys was on an e-bike and that the alleged victim had told them to slow down before an argument escalated. title: US-Israel Missile Strikes Spark Chaos Across Middle East | 10 News
description: Iranian missiles and Israeli interceptors were seen flying and exploding over the skies of Tel Aviv overnight.  It comes as the United States and Israel launched their most ambitious attack on Iran in decades, with US President Donald Trump stating Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed during the operation.  Neighbouring countries have also been caught up in the strikes, with reports of missile attacks in Bahrain, Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Doha, and Riyadh.  Meanwhile, major airports, including Dubai International Airport and Doha Airport, have been affected, leaving thousands of travellers stranded across the region. title: Thousands Of Travellers Stranded As Dubai Airspace Closes Amid Strikes | 10 News
description: Thousands have been left stranded inside Dubai International Airport due to airspace closures amid escalating military activity in the region.  The suspension of flight operations at major hubs has triggered widespread travel disruption, with services cancelled or re-routed.  The chaos comes as conflict unfolds in Iran and at military bases in neighbouring countries, with reports of airports in Abu Dhabi, Dubai and Kuwait being targeted.  Australians are being urged to avoid travel to the region, and those already there are advised to follow official instructions and take shelter where necessary. title: Explosions Erupt Across Middle East As US-Israel Strikes Kill Iran’s Supreme Leader | 10 News
description: Explosions continue across the Middle East nearly a day after Israel and the United States launched joint strikes on Iran, killing hundreds, including the country’s Supreme Leader.  The first attacks hit just before 9:30 a.m. local time, with blasts reported across Tehran and other areas. A primary school near a military target was reportedly struck, leaving dozens dead.  Iran responded swiftly, firing on regional US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, and the UAE.  The strikes have disrupted global travel, leaving Australians among thousands stranded abroad. title: Airports Bombed, Aussies Left Scrambling, Iran's Supreme Leader Dead, Trump's Warning | 10 News+
description: Iran has confirmed the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei after a massive US-Israeli attack that US President Donald Trump said would continue with heavy bombing if Iran continues to retaliate.  Iran is striking back with a fresh wave of attacks across the region after the supreme leader’s killing. It has already unleashed strikes on US military bases, Israel and targets across the Middle East, damaging air hubs, rocking densely populated areas and disrupting oil shipments.  The contrast of celebrations and mourning highlights deep divides in Iran. Across the world, people took to the streets, with some celebrating and others protesting the strikes on Iran.
